title: Process-first target contract for Identity, Tenant, RBAC and Admin Non-Bypass

authority.route_evolution_policy: No new route or screen split is authorized by this contract.
out_of_scope: new product scope outside True-UX route evolution authority

supporting_routes_not_new_scope.reason: BP-104 security configuration naturally maps to existing route metadata, but EPIC-06 target screen list does not authorize a new implementation target there.
BP-109 team assignment naturally maps to the existing tenant team route, but EPIC-06 target screen list does not authorize a new implementation target there.

target_screen_contract.decision: EPIC-06 implementation must preserve the masterplan target screen list. S010 and S016 may be referenced as supporting proof only; product changes there require a later route-evolution record.

role_guard_contract.route_shell_access: Route visibility never grants action, payload or mutation authority.
admin_and_security: may manage scoped governance surfaces where explicitly allowed
must not approve, generate, download or share restricted export packages
must not view internal advice payload by governance authority alone
compliance_officer: may perform compliance-owned release or evidence sufficiency actions only with tenant and object scope
must still pass audit and payload visibility checks
client_success: may support tenant setup and invitations where explicitly allowed
must not manage sensitive role or policy changes without governance role authority
external_or_limited_roles: must not view internal-only documents, evidence, audit or recommendation payloads

payload_visibility_contract.acceptance: Any EPIC-06 API or command response that reaches UI must be explicitly client-safe or explicitly internal-only with no client release.

audit_evidence_contract.failure_mode: Audit persistence failure blocks sensitive mutation before state change.
audit_evidence_contract.proof_rule: Every process marked implemented must name at least one positive proof and one negative proof in the EPIC-06 matrix update.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Authenticated demo actor resolves into actor, role, tenant and route boundary context.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Missing tenant context, actor-role mismatch or actor-tenant mismatch fails closed before payload or mutation authority.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Scoped invitation action routes through tenant governance command spine and records audit.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Opening or cancelling invite drawer does not execute workflow mutation or imply role activation.

process_contracts.service_boundary: demo session and profile/current-user API boundary
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Identity/profile context is resolved before route/action/payload evaluation.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Profile or role context mismatch cannot activate downstream access.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Tenant membership assignment has explicit tenant scope and audit proof.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Cross-tenant or unsupported membership action is denied before mutation.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Role assignment requires acknowledgement, exact second confirmation, tenant scope and audit.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Drawer open/cancel or wrong confirmation phrase does not mutate role state or imply access expansion.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Allowed scoped role/action/object decisions return `DEMO_ROLE_AWARE_ALLOW` with required audit/review flags.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Forbidden role, action, tenant or object combinations return explicit deny reason codes.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Object-scoped action is allowed only when object type, tenant and object id match current scope.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Missing object target, missing object scope, type mismatch, tenant mismatch or id mismatch fails closed.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Access request approval requires scoped acknowledgement and routes through audit-backed command spine.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Drawer open/cancel/escalate/deny does not submit scoped approval mutation.

process_contracts.service_boundary: permissionEngine requiresSecondConfirmation plus UI exact phrase lifecycle
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Sensitive ASSIGN/REVOKE/RELEASE/MANAGE actions require second confirmation.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Wrong phrase or unacknowledged drawer cannot submit mutation.

process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Admin/security may manage scoped governance where allowed.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Admin/security cannot bypass advisor approval, evidence sufficiency, client visibility, export/redaction, internal advice payload or audit persistence.

process_contracts.service_boundary: typed workflow and tenant governance audit persistence
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Denied sensitive attempt records or exposes audit-required proof before any state claim.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Audit persistence unavailable returns fail-closed error and commandExecuted false.

process_contracts.service_boundary: platform settings work surface plus permissionEngine MANAGE PLATFORM
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Platform settings admin surface names one page job and guarded next action.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Platform settings visibility does not grant payload release, export, evidence sufficiency or audit bypass.

process_contracts.service_boundary: permissionEngine MANAGE POLICY/ROLE plus route registry metadata
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Security configuration proof is limited to existing route metadata and shared guard behavior unless a later route-evolution record promotes S010 into target scope.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Security/admin role cannot use configuration authority to bypass protected downstream gates.

process_contracts.service_boundary: tenant policy work surface plus permissionEngine MANAGE PERMISSION/POLICY
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Tenant policy change remains tenant-scoped and audit-bound.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Policy surface cannot bypass compliance release, export/redaction, evidence sufficiency or audit.

process_contracts.service_boundary: evidence template work surface plus permissionEngine MANAGE EVIDENCE_RECORD
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Evidence template administration can configure templates without marking evidence sufficient.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Admin/security cannot force evidence sufficiency from template/admin authority.

process_contracts.service_boundary: export template work surface plus permissionEngine EXPORT_REQUEST guards
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Export/redaction templates can be configured without generating or sharing restricted exports.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Admin/security cannot bypass export approval, redaction or client-visibility gates.

process_contracts.service_boundary: policy versioning proof must be explicit; current matrix says specified_only
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Policy/version change has visible configure, validate, confirm, audit and apply-to-flows proof.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Draft/configuration state cannot be overclaimed as applied policy or downstream release authority.

process_contracts.service_boundary: j06.assignTeam plus tenant setup work surfaces
process_contracts.positive_acceptance: Team assignment is tenant-scoped and audit-bound through the existing command spine.
process_contracts.negative_acceptance: Team assignment does not grant advisor, compliance, export, evidence or client visibility authority.

EPIC-06-IMPL-01A.contract: Primary area hub/workbench entry must make S048 the governance process hub with one page job, one next action, no downstream overclaim and screenshot proof.

EPIC-06-IMPL-01B.contract: Core queue/detail/step surfaces must cover S049/S050 role and access-request flows with explicit lifecycle states.

EPIC-06-IMPL-01C.contract: Proof/audit/client-safe boundary must keep concise user-facing proof while preserving fail-closed blockers.

EPIC-06-IMPL-01D.contract: Coverage matrix update must mark only implemented/proven steps and retain partial/missing states where proof is absent.

positive_paths: Authorized scoped user can open target route shell without receiving mutation authority by route visibility alone.
Authorized scoped governance command executes through /api/tenant-governance/actions, writes audit proof and returns clientVisible false.
Role and access request flows require acknowledgement and second confirmation where sensitive.
Compliance-owned gates remain compliance-owned after governance/admin interaction.
Worksurface shell shows one page job, active step and safety boundary for every changed target surface.
negative_paths: Unsupported governance action returns INVALID_REQUEST with commandExecuted false.
Missing database/audit persistence fails closed for governance commands.
Cross-tenant, missing tenant, actor-role mismatch, actor-tenant mismatch, missing object target and object scope mismatch deny before mutation.
Admin/security cannot force evidence sufficiency, client visibility, export, internal advice payload or audit suppression.
Open/cancel/escape/escalate/deny UI interactions do not submit mutation unless the specific command is confirmed.

notes: S010 and S016 are supporting proof routes only.
If product implementation on S010/S016 is desired, create a later route-evolution decision record before editing those surfaces.
